2606.22485.title: VADAOrchestra: Neurosymbolic Orchestration of Adaptive Reasoning Workflows
2606.22485.paper_summary: Decision-making in real-world settings rarely follows a fixed script. Instead, it unfolds as a dynamic reasoning process in which the appropriate course of action evolves as new context and data become available. Traditional Business Process Management systems provide rigor, determinism, and auditability, yet they generally struggle to adapt their execution at runtime. Conversely, agentic systems based on Large Language Models (LLMs) bring flexibility to decision-making, but they are inherently opaque, often unreliable, and suffer from significant scalability constraints when operating over large datasets. To combine these complementary paradigms, we introduce VADAOrchestra, a neurosymbolic framework that models complex workflows as evolving reasoning processes. The framework adopts a hybrid approach: given a user query and a collection of data sources, an LLM-based orchestrator incrementally plans and adapts the workflow. This is encoded as a logic program in a fragment of Datalog+/- where predicates correspond to tool invocations and rules represent both predefined domain dependencies and logic constructs synthesized on demand to manipulate intermediate results. All logical inference tasks are then executed by a state-of-the-art Datalog+/- symbolic engine. This approach provides a verifiable reasoning trace, supporting the auditability and reproducibility of the entire process. Furthermore, by decoupling high-level orchestration from symbolic inference, it addresses scalability concerns, enabling complex reasoning over large datasets through targeted data querying. We evaluate VADAOrchestra on real-world financial use cases, demonstrating faithfulness, scalability, and explainability compared to standard agentic architectures.
2606.22485.paper_summary_zh: 在現實世界的環境中，決策過程很少遵循固定的腳本。相反，它展現為一個動態的推理過程，其中適當的行動方案隨著新情境和數據的出現而演變。傳統的商業流程管理系統提供了嚴謹性、確定性和可審計性，但它們通常難以在運行時調整其執行。相對而言，基於大型語言模型（LLMs）的代理系統為決策過程帶來了靈活性，但它們本質上是模糊的，經常不可靠，並在處理大型數據集時面臨顯著的可擴展性限制。為了結合這些互補的範式，我們介紹了VADAOrchestra，一個神經符號框架，將複雜的工作流程建模為不斷演變的推理過程。該框架採用混合方法：給定用戶查詢和一組數據來源，基於LLM的調度器逐步規劃和調整工作流程。這被編碼為Datalog+/-的一個邏輯程序，其中謂詞對應於工具調用，而規則則表示預定義的領域依賴性和根據需求合成的邏輯結構，以操縱中間結果。然後，所有邏輯推理任務都由最先進的Datalog+/-符號引擎執行。這種方法提供了一個可驗證的推理痕跡，支持整個過程的可審計性和可重現性。此外，通過將高級調度與符號推理解耦，它解決了可擴展性問題，通過針對性數據查詢實現對大型數據集的複雜推理。我們在現實世界的金融用例上評估了VADAOrchestra，展示了與標準代理架構相比的忠實性、可擴展性和可解釋性。
2606.22485.author: Teodoro Baldazzi et.al.
2606.22485.authors: Teodoro Baldazzi, Luigi Bellomarini, Andrea Coletta, Michela Iezzi, Carsten Maple, Alessandro Pesare, Emanuel Sallinger
